    "R1.2": "Several internal logical tensions are present and assessable: IC peaks at 3.42 V and 3.58 V are ascribed to graphite anode staging, yet LFP is included in the corpus — LFP's IC peaks differ substantially, putting the auxiliary-token design in tension with the chemistry-agnostic claim. Logical chain from 'attention weights adaptively' to 'no retraining needed' is asserted without mechanistic justification.",
    "R1.3": "Specific quantitative claims are made (RMSE 1.8% vs 3.4%/2.9%/2.5%, peak near 3.42 V as strongest single-feature correlate). An opinion on the work requires assessing whether these numbers are plausibly supported or whether the experimental setup (mixed-cell training, held-out transfer) actually tests the stated advantages.",
    "R1.4": "The paper positions itself against Plett (2004) EKF, Richardson et al. (2017) GP, and a BiLSTM. Evaluating 'what do you think' on a research paper centrally requires judging whether the architectural choices and cross-chemistry transfer constitute a genuine advance over these baselines or a repackaging of existing ideas."
  },

rationale.R1.1: The artifact makes explicit contribution claims (chemistry-agnostic operation, attention-based adaptive weighting, encoder transfer without retraining) and a gap statement (conventional approaches require fitted models). Any opinion on the work centrally implicates whether these claims are coherently stated and well-scoped.
rationale.R1.2: Several internal logical tensions are present and assessable: IC peaks at 3.42 V and 3.58 V are ascribed to graphite anode staging, yet LFP is included in the corpus — LFP's IC peaks differ substantially, putting the auxiliary-token design in tension with the chemistry-agnostic claim. Logical chain from 'attention weights adaptively' to 'no retraining needed' is asserted without mechanistic justification.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are made (RMSE 1.8% vs 3.4%/2.9%/2.5%, peak near 3.42 V as strongest single-feature correlate). An opinion on the work requires assessing whether these numbers are plausibly supported or whether the experimental setup (mixed-cell training, held-out transfer) actually tests the stated advantages.
rationale.R1.4: The paper positions itself against Plett (2004) EKF, Richardson et al. (2017) GP, and a BiLSTM. Evaluating 'what do you think' on a research paper centrally requires judging whether the architectural choices and cross-chemistry transfer constitute a genuine advance over these baselines or a repackaging of existing ideas.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review be primarily a scientific and technical critique (evaluating the soundness of claims, novelty relative to prior work, and evidence quality), or primarily a writing and presentation review (evaluating clarity, structure, and prose)?
